summary: @aonunited/angular preinstall exfiltrates host identifiers to webhook.site and oast.fun
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall script (preinstall.js) collects the installer's hostname, OS username, current working directory, and a timestamp, then sends them to two attacker-style out-of-band endpoints: a DNS lookup of a subdomain under `oast.fun` (an Interactsh-style callback service) constructed from the installer's identifiers, and an HTTPS POST of a JSON payload to a `webhook.site` URL. The package itself provides no functionality — it is a high-version (99.0.1) namespace-confusion lure against an internal `@aonunited/angular` package, designed so that any environment whose resolver picks the public registry copy will auto-execute the beacon. Although the metadata describes this as authorized HackerOne VDP research, the package is published to the public npm registry, so any developer or build system that installs or mistypes it leaks host fingerprints to third-party infrastructure without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
